questions.stem:
—Have you finished your homework yet?
—Yes, I ______ it an hour ago. I ______ it for 2 hours.
questions.explanation: 本题考查现在完成时与一般过去时的区别。第一空后有'an hour ago'（一小时前），这是明确的过去时间状语，必须用一般过去时'finished'；第二空有'for 2 hours'（持续两小时），表示动作从过去开始持续到现在，用现在完成时'have done'。技巧：看到'ago'用过去时，看到'for/since'用完成时。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: The boy ______ won the first prize is called Bosco. He is in Class 3, Grade 8.
questions.explanation: 本题考查定语从句关系词的选择。先行词是'the boy'（指人），且关系词在从句中作主语（won the first prize），所以用'who'。'which'用于指物；'whom'用于指人但作宾语；'whose'表示所属关系（谁的）。口诀：人物who/that，物which/that，主格who，宾格whom。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: The new library ______ by the end of next month. It will be open to all students.
questions.explanation: 本题考查一般将来时的被动语态。主语'the new library'（新图书馆）是动作'complete'（完成）的承受者，所以用被动语态；时间状语'by the end of next month'（下月底前）表示将来，所以用'will be completed'。结构：will + be + 过去分词。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem:
—Could you tell me ______?
—Sure. He lives in Nanshan District, Shenzhen.
questions.explanation: 本题考查宾语从句的语序和时态。宾语从句必须用陈述句语序（主语+谓语），排除A和C；根据答语'He lives...'（一般现在时）可知问的是现在的居住情况，用一般现在时，排除D（过去时）。技巧：宾从用陈述语序，时态随主句变化（主句现在时，从句根据实际时间）。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: If you are not ______ with your exam results, you should make a better plan for next term.
questions.explanation: 本题考查形容词辨析。主语是'you'（人），表示'感到满意的'用'satisfied'；'exciting/excited'（兴奋的）不符合句意；'satisfying'（令人满意的）通常修饰物。固定搭配：be satisfied with（对……满意）。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.stem: As teenagers, we should ______ good ideas to solve the environmental problems in our city.
questions.explanation: 本题考查动词短语辨析。'come up with'意为'想出（主意、办法）'；'catch up with'意为'追上，赶上'；'keep up with'意为'跟上，保持同步'；'put up with'意为'忍受'。根据句意'想出好主意'，选A。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.stem: It is ______ to drive after drinking alcohol. You may lose your driver's license.
questions.explanation: 本题考查词性转换及前缀。be动词后接形容词作表语，排除C（副词）和D（名词）；根据常识，酒后驾车是'非法的'，'legal'（合法的）加前缀'il-'构成反义词'illegal'（非法的）。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school started a "Green Campus" project. All students were encouraged to take part in it. The goal was to reduce waste and protect the environment.

At first, I didn't think it would make much difference. (8)______, as time went by, I changed my mind. My classmates and I collected plastic bottles and used paper every day. We also set up special bins for different kinds of waste.

One day, I noticed that the playground was (9)______ with litter after lunch. Instead of walking away, I picked up the rubbish and put it into the bin. Soon, some other students joined me. (10)______, we cleaned the whole area in ten minutes.

This experience taught me that small actions can (11)______ big changes. If everyone does their part, we can make our school a better place. The project has not only improved our environment but also (12)______ our sense of responsibility.
questions.explanation: 考查逻辑关系。前句说'起初我认为没什么不同'，后句说'随着时间推移我改变了想法'，前后是转折关系，用'However'（然而）。'Therefore'（因此）表因果；'Besides'（此外）表递进；'Otherwise'（否则）表条件。
questions.label: 完形填空

questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school started a "Green Campus" project. All students were encouraged to take part in it. The goal was to reduce waste and protect the environment.

At first, I didn't think it would make much difference. (8)______, as time went by, I changed my mind. My classmates and I collected plastic bottles and used paper every day. We also set up special bins for different kinds of waste.

One day, I noticed that the playground was (9)______ with litter after lunch. Instead of walking away, I picked up the rubbish and put it into the bin. Soon, some other students joined me. (10)______, we cleaned the whole area in ten minutes.

This experience taught me that small actions can (11)______ big changes. If everyone does their part, we can make our school a better place. The project has not only improved our environment but also (12)______ our sense of responsibility.
questions.explanation: 考查固定搭配。'be covered with'是固定短语，意为'被……覆盖'，句意为'操场被垃圾覆盖'。'be filled with'（充满）虽然意思接近，但通常指容器被液体或气体充满，垃圾覆盖地面用'covered'更准确；'pleased'（高兴的）和'strict'（严格的）不符合语境。
questions.label: 完形填空

questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school started a "Green Campus" project. All students were encouraged to take part in it. The goal was to reduce waste and protect the environment.

At first, I didn't think it would make much difference. (8)______, as time went by, I changed my mind. My classmates and I collected plastic bottles and used paper every day. We also set up special bins for different kinds of waste.

One day, I noticed that the playground was (9)______ with litter after lunch. Instead of walking away, I picked up the rubbish and put it into the bin. Soon, some other students joined me. (10)______, we cleaned the whole area in ten minutes.

This experience taught me that small actions can (11)______ big changes. If everyone does their part, we can make our school a better place. The project has not only improved our environment but also (12)______ our sense of responsibility.
questions.explanation: 考查语境推理。前文提到'我捡起垃圾，其他同学加入'，后文说'十分钟内打扫了整个区域'，这是最终的结果，用'Finally'（最后，终于）。'Luckily'（幸运地）强调运气；'Suddenly'（突然）强调意外；'Recently'（最近）是时间副词，不符合此处逻辑。
questions.label: 完形填空

questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school started a "Green Campus" project. All students were encouraged to take part in it. The goal was to reduce waste and protect the environment.

At first, I didn't think it would make much difference. (8)______, as time went by, I changed my mind. My classmates and I collected plastic bottles and used paper every day. We also set up special bins for different kinds of waste.

One day, I noticed that the playground was (9)______ with litter after lunch. Instead of walking away, I picked up the rubbish and put it into the bin. Soon, some other students joined me. (10)______, we cleaned the whole area in ten minutes.

This experience taught me that small actions can (11)______ big changes. If everyone does their part, we can make our school a better place. The project has not only improved our environment but also (12)______ our sense of responsibility.
questions.explanation: 考查固定搭配。'bring changes'意为'带来变化'，符合句意'小行动能带来大变化'。'make changes'虽然也有'做出改变'的意思，但主语通常是人（we make changes），而此处主语是'actions'，用'bring'（带来）更自然；'take'和'give'与changes搭配不当。
questions.label: 完形填空

questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school started a "Green Campus" project. All students were encouraged to take part in it. The goal was to reduce waste and protect the environment.

At first, I didn't think it would make much difference. (8)______, as time went by, I changed my mind. My classmates and I collected plastic bottles and used paper every day. We also set up special bins for different kinds of waste.

One day, I noticed that the playground was (9)______ with litter after lunch. Instead of walking away, I picked up the rubbish and put it into the bin. Soon, some other students joined me. (10)______, we cleaned the whole area in ten minutes.

This experience taught me that small actions can (11)______ big changes. If everyone does their part, we can make our school a better place. The project has not only improved our environment but also (12)______ our sense of responsibility.
questions.explanation: 考查动词辨析。'develop a sense of responsibility'是固定搭配，意为'培养责任感'。'increase'（增加）通常指数量；'train'（训练）主语通常是人；'practice'（练习）不符合语境。
questions.label: 完形填空

questions.group_passage:
In today's digital world, many teenagers spend hours looking at screens every day. This has led to a rapid increase in eye problems among young people. Dr. Chen, a famous eye specialist from Shenzhen Hospital, has offered some useful advice.

Dr. Chen suggests following the "20-20-20" rule. Every 20 minutes, look at something 20 feet (about 6 meters) away for at least 20 seconds. This simple habit helps relax the eye muscles and reduces tiredness. "When we stare at screens, we tend to blink less often, which makes our eyes dry," Dr. Chen explained.

Besides the 20-20-20 rule, proper lighting is also important. Reading in dim light or in a moving vehicle can cause serious damage to your eyes. Moreover, outdoor activities play a key role in eye health. Studies show that students who spend two hours outside every day have lower rates of myopia (近视).

However, many students ignore these warnings until they have to wear glasses. Prevention is always better than treatment. As Dr. Chen says, "Your eyes are precious. Take care of them before it's too late."
questions.stem: According to the "20-20-20" rule mentioned in the passage, what should we do?
options: A. Exercise for 20 minutes every day
B. Look at screens for only 20 seconds
C. Look at something 6 meters away every 20 minutes for 20 seconds
D. Spend 20 hours outdoors every week
questions.explanation: 细节理解题。根据第二段第二句'Every 20 minutes, look at something 20 feet (about 6 meters) away for at least 20 seconds'可知，每20分钟看20英尺（约6米）外的物体至少20秒。选项C准确概括了这一规则。
questions.label: 阅读-细节理解

questions.group_passage:
In today's digital world, many teenagers spend hours looking at screens every day. This has led to a rapid increase in eye problems among young people. Dr. Chen, a famous eye specialist from Shenzhen Hospital, has offered some useful advice.

Dr. Chen suggests following the "20-20-20" rule. Every 20 minutes, look at something 20 feet (about 6 meters) away for at least 20 seconds. This simple habit helps relax the eye muscles and reduces tiredness. "When we stare at screens, we tend to blink less often, which makes our eyes dry," Dr. Chen explained.

Besides the 20-20-20 rule, proper lighting is also important. Reading in dim light or in a moving vehicle can cause serious damage to your eyes. Moreover, outdoor activities play a key role in eye health. Studies show that students who spend two hours outside every day have lower rates of myopia (近视).

However, many students ignore these warnings until they have to wear glasses. Prevention is always better than treatment. As Dr. Chen says, "Your eyes are precious. Take care of them before it's too late."
questions.stem: What does the underlined word "stare" in Paragraph 2 probably mean?
options: B. look at closely without moving
questions.explanation: 词义猜测题。根据上下文'When we stare at screens, we tend to blink less often'（当我们stare屏幕时，我们往往眨眼次数减少），可以推断'stare'是指'长时间盯着看'，即'look at closely without moving'。'look for'（寻找）；'look after'（照顾）；'look around'（环顾）均不符合语境。
questions.label: 阅读-词义猜测

questions.group_passage:
In today's digital world, many teenagers spend hours looking at screens every day. This has led to a rapid increase in eye problems among young people. Dr. Chen, a famous eye specialist from Shenzhen Hospital, has offered some useful advice.

Dr. Chen suggests following the "20-20-20" rule. Every 20 minutes, look at something 20 feet (about 6 meters) away for at least 20 seconds. This simple habit helps relax the eye muscles and reduces tiredness. "When we stare at screens, we tend to blink less often, which makes our eyes dry," Dr. Chen explained.

Besides the 20-20-20 rule, proper lighting is also important. Reading in dim light or in a moving vehicle can cause serious damage to your eyes. Moreover, outdoor activities play a key role in eye health. Studies show that students who spend two hours outside every day have lower rates of myopia (近视).

However, many students ignore these warnings until they have to wear glasses. Prevention is always better than treatment. As Dr. Chen says, "Your eyes are precious. Take care of them before it's too late."
questions.stem: What can we infer from the passage?
options: A. All students who use screens will get myopia
B. Wearing glasses is the best way to solve eye problems
C. Spending time outdoors can help protect our eyes
D. Dr. Chen thinks teenagers should not use digital devices
questions.explanation: 推理判断题。根据第三段最后一句'Studies show that students who spend two hours outside every day have lower rates of myopia'（研究表明每天户外两小时的学生近视率更低），可推断户外活动有助于保护视力，选C。A项'all'过于绝对；B项与文中'Prevention is always better than treatment'矛盾；D项文中未提及禁止使用电子设备。
questions.label: 阅读-推理判断

questions.group_passage:
In today's digital world, many teenagers spend hours looking at screens every day. This has led to a rapid increase in eye problems among young people. Dr. Chen, a famous eye specialist from Shenzhen Hospital, has offered some useful advice.

Dr. Chen suggests following the "20-20-20" rule. Every 20 minutes, look at something 20 feet (about 6 meters) away for at least 20 seconds. This simple habit helps relax the eye muscles and reduces tiredness. "When we stare at screens, we tend to blink less often, which makes our eyes dry," Dr. Chen explained.

Besides the 20-20-20 rule, proper lighting is also important. Reading in dim light or in a moving vehicle can cause serious damage to your eyes. Moreover, outdoor activities play a key role in eye health. Studies show that students who spend two hours outside every day have lower rates of myopia (近视).

However, many students ignore these warnings until they have to wear glasses. Prevention is always better than treatment. As Dr. Chen says, "Your eyes are precious. Take care of them before it's too late."
questions.stem: What is the best title for this passage?
options: B. How to Protect Your Eyes
D. The Danger of Digital Devices
questions.explanation: 主旨大意题。文章主要介绍了眼科医生陈医生提出的保护视力的建议，包括20-20-20规则、适当照明和户外活动等。A项'眼镜的历史'文中未提及；C项'陈医生的职业生涯'只是细节；D项'数码设备的危险'过于片面。B项'如何保护眼睛'最能概括全文主旨。
questions.label: 阅读-主旨大意

questions.group_passage:
Many students struggle to balance schoolwork and hobbies. They often focus too much on grades and ignore their interests. (17)______ This makes them feel unhappy and stressed.

The good news is that you can do both well with good time management. Start by making a list of your daily tasks. (18)______ Put the most important tasks first, but also leave some time for relaxation.

Also, remember that hobbies are not a waste of time. (19)______ They can help you relax and even improve your creativity. When you go back to studying, you will feel refreshed.

(20)______ Finding the right balance takes time, but it is worth the effort. You will not only get good grades but also enjoy your teenage years.
options: A. However, with a good plan, you can enjoy both study and hobbies.
B. In fact, they are good for your mental health and study efficiency.
C. As a result, they miss out on fun activities and feel burned out.
D. Then, decide how much time each activity needs.
E. On the other hand, some students spend too much time on hobbies and fail their exams.
questions.explanation: 考查逻辑衔接。前句说'他们太关注成绩而忽略兴趣'，后句说'这让他们不开心和压力大'，中间需要表示结果的句子。C项'As a result, they miss out on fun activities and feel burned out'（结果，他们错过了有趣的活动，感到精疲力竭）承上启下，符合逻辑。
questions.label: 短文填空

questions.group_passage:
Many students struggle to balance schoolwork and hobbies. They often focus too much on grades and ignore their interests. (17)______ This makes them feel unhappy and stressed.

The good news is that you can do both well with good time management. Start by making a list of your daily tasks. (18)______ Put the most important tasks first, but also leave some time for relaxation.

Also, remember that hobbies are not a waste of time. (19)______ They can help you relax and even improve your creativity. When you go back to studying, you will feel refreshed.

(20)______ Finding the right balance takes time, but it is worth the effort. You will not only get good grades but also enjoy your teenage years.
options: A. However, with a good plan, you can enjoy both study and hobbies.
B. In fact, they are good for your mental health and study efficiency.
C. As a result, they miss out on fun activities and feel burned out.
D. Then, decide how much time each activity needs.
E. On the other hand, some students spend too much time on hobbies and fail their exams.
questions.explanation: 考查顺序连接。前句说'首先列出每日任务清单'，后句说'把最重要的任务放在首位'，中间需要表示下一步动作的句子。D项'Then, decide how much time each activity needs'（然后，决定每项活动需要多少时间）符合'then'表示的顺序逻辑。
questions.label: 短文填空

questions.group_passage:
Many students struggle to balance schoolwork and hobbies. They often focus too much on grades and ignore their interests. (17)______ This makes them feel unhappy and stressed.

The good news is that you can do both well with good time management. Start by making a list of your daily tasks. (18)______ Put the most important tasks first, but also leave some time for relaxation.

Also, remember that hobbies are not a waste of time. (19)______ They can help you relax and even improve your creativity. When you go back to studying, you will feel refreshed.

(20)______ Finding the right balance takes time, but it is worth the effort. You will not only get good grades but also enjoy your teenage years.
options: A. However, with a good plan, you can enjoy both study and hobbies.
B. In fact, they are good for your mental health and study efficiency.
C. As a result, they miss out on fun activities and feel burned out.
D. Then, decide how much time each activity needs.
E. On the other hand, some students spend too much time on hobbies and fail their exams.
questions.explanation: 考查递进解释。前句说'爱好不是浪费时间'，后句说'它们能帮助你放松甚至提高创造力'，中间需要进一步解释爱好好处的句子。B项'In fact, they are good for your mental health and study efficiency'（事实上，它们对你的心理健康和学习效率都有好处）起到递进说明的作用。
questions.label: 短文填空

questions.group_passage:
Many students struggle to balance schoolwork and hobbies. They often focus too much on grades and ignore their interests. (17)______ This makes them feel unhappy and stressed.

The good news is that you can do both well with good time management. Start by making a list of your daily tasks. (18)______ Put the most important tasks first, but also leave some time for relaxation.

Also, remember that hobbies are not a waste of time. (19)______ They can help you relax and even improve your creativity. When you go back to studying, you will feel refreshed.

(20)______ Finding the right balance takes time, but it is worth the effort. You will not only get good grades but also enjoy your teenage years.
options: A. However, with a good plan, you can enjoy both study and hobbies.
B. In fact, they are good for your mental health and study efficiency.
C. As a result, they miss out on fun activities and feel burned out.
D. Then, decide how much time each activity needs.
E. On the other hand, some students spend too much time on hobbies and fail their exams.
questions.explanation: 考查转折总结。后句说'找到正确的平衡需要时间，但值得努力'，前句需要引出这一结论的过渡句。A项'However, with a good plan, you can enjoy both study and hobbies'（然而，有了好的计划，你可以同时享受学习和爱好）与后文形成转折递进关系，符合段落结尾总结全文的逻辑。
questions.label: 短文填空
